summary: Verified FFMPEG NameError regression fix in backend/rendering.py. End-to-end video pipeline runs cleanly without any FFMPEG NameError. Created video vid_39e938c98e0b for e2e_render@test.com — pipeline advanced through script -> stock -> voiceover -> thumbnails -> SEO -> rendering (progress 88 within ~85s) and reached READY (progress 100) within ~3 minutes. get_ffmpeg_binaries() returns two absolute existing paths (static_ffmpeg linux_arm64 build).
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is correct and minimal: `_run` now resolves ffmpeg binary via get_ffmpeg_binaries() (static_ffmpeg fallback) and auto-prepends it if the caller did not pass an absolute path (line 34-35). `_normalize_video` no longer contains the bare `FFMPEG` identifier.
Both `_normalize_video`/`_normalize_image` and the two concat calls in `render_video_file` pass argv WITHOUT the binary as arg[0]; the auto-prepend logic in `_run` handles this consistently.

Minor: the check `cmd[0].startswith('/')` is a heuristic to detect an already-supplied absolute binary path. It works today (no caller passes a non-absolute binary), but a comment or explicit flag would be safer for future edits.

Note (pre-existing, not part of this fix scope): the iteration_1 critical issues (Pexels validator only checks status 200; media files for vid_3e10b384068f missing on disk after pod restart) remain open — but they are outside the FFMPEG bug and were explicitly out of scope for this review.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 video vid_39e938c98e0b via POST /api/videos/create (consumed the user's remaining video_credit and Emergent LLM budget as noted in test_credentials.md). Final status: READY, progress 100.
context_for_next_testing_agent: FFMPEG NameError regression is fixed and verified. Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_ffmpeg_regression.py -v` to re-verify. Test file uses cookie-session auth (login sets a session cookie; there is no `access_token` field in the /api/auth/login response — it returns {user, message}). The pipeline test skips gracefully if credits are exhausted. User e2e_render@test.com now has 0 video_credits after this run.
rca of the issue: Original: rendering.py:_normalize_video referenced a bare identifier FFMPEG that was never assigned, and _run passed a command list that did not include the ffmpeg binary in argv[0]. Reproduction (pre-fix): call generate_video_pipeline -> render step -> _normalize_video -> raises NameError('name FFMPEG is not defined'), which is caught by the outer try/except in generate_video_pipeline and stored in video.error_message with the toast 'Video generation failed: name FFMPEG is not defined'. Fix verified: _run now calls get_ffmpeg_binaries() (which lazily resolves via shutil.which or static_ffmpeg's get_or_fetch_platform_executables_else_raise()) and auto-prepends the resolved absolute ffmpeg path when the caller did not supply an absolute binary. Confirmed on this container (no system ffmpeg) that static_ffmpeg returns /root/.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/static_ffmpeg/bin/linux_arm64/{ffmpeg,ffprobe} and full pipeline succeeds end-to-end.
